title: Who Was Mother Teresa, Really?
description: This session introduces the key theme briefly, then lets Mother Teresa answer in her own words through recorded interviews.
points: Not merely a symbol of kindness, but a radical disciple.
Deeply loved, widely criticized, and often misunderstood.
Uniquely relevant because we can still hear her directly.
quote: We will let her speak for herself.

title: Brief Timeline
description: Her holiness unfolded in the modern world, not in a distant century. Her mission grew from obedience, not institutional strategy.

title: The Radical Leap
description: She left the safety of convent life and entered the slums with no financial blueprint or worldly guarantee of success.

title: Fr. Gabri on Her Simplicity
description: Fr. Gabri interprets what made her unusual: deep certainty that God was with her.
points: A saint is not defined only by miracles.
A saint faces hardship with peace.
Simplicity with God is hard to live.

title: Love That Hurts
description: True love costs something. It takes flesh in sacrifice and self-gift.
points: Where do we prefer comfort to love?
quote: If we really want to love God, we must give until it hurts.

title: Sharing, Not Merely Donating
description: She wanted people to come close enough to the poor that love became personal.
points: The poor are our brothers and sisters.

title: The Hunger for Love
description: Loneliness and being unwanted can be more destructive than material poverty.
points: The West has its own form of poverty.

title: Dignity of Every Person
description: Her first act was to restore the dignity the world had stripped away.
points: Hunger is not only for bread.
The person in the street is a child of God.

title: One Person at a Time
description: She refused to wait for global solutions, focusing instead on personal charity.
points: Charity begins with the one before us.

title: Love for Jesus
description: The center of her mission was Christ. She saw Him in the hungry and dying.
points: A response to Jesus in the poor.

title: Why Criticisms Exist
description: Objections often come from a clash of worldviews: secular versus spiritual.
points: Understand the lens of the critic.
quote: A fair treatment begins by understanding the lens.

title: Root Causes
description: Clarifying her vocation to care for the person in front of her now.

title: Exploiting Poverty?
description: She rejected treating the suffering like a 'zoo' or spectacle.

title: Abortion and Dignity
description: Conviction paired with action: adoption, welcome, and care.
points: Every child is wanted by God.

title: Other Religions
description: Those in her care remained free to practice their own burial customs and faiths.
points: Serving all as children of God.

title: Suffering
description: Bringing dignity and love into suffering rather than just endurance.
points: Sharing in the passion of Christ.

title: Providence
description: Refusing ordinary fundraising to depend radically on God's providence.

title: Spiritual Darkness
description: A prolonged interior darkness, yet total perseverance in service.
quote: Faith is not the same as emotional certainty.

title: What is a Saint?
description: Holiness is steadfast union with God in difficulty.

title: What This Means for Us
description: Closing with examination: how can we start with one person and one sacrifice?
quote: Where is God asking me to step out?
